response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 20+ years of broad software development experience, strong open-source engagement, and genuine full-stack depth — but critically fails the German language requirement, listing only 'Basic' proficiency against the stated C1 minimum. For an IT consulting role requiring direct client advisory in Germany, this is a hard disqualifier. Beyond the language gap, the resume shows little evidence of formal team leadership, project management ownership, UML/architectural modeling work, or IT security and compliance experience, all of which are explicitly required for this Head of Development position.
strengths: 20+ years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS domains with genuine breadth in languages and platforms
Strong open-source culture and Linux exposure demonstrated through GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial, and FOSS4G speaking engagement
Community teaching instinct evidenced by recurring workshops, Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and a Selenium course — signals ability to mentor junior developers
concerns: German listed as 'Basic', far below the C1 minimum required for client advisory and team leadership in a German-market consulting firm
No documented formal leadership, team management, or P&L/project ownership experience — roles are uniformly individual contributor despite seniority titles
No mention of UML, formal IT architecture methodologies, requirements management, or security/risk/compliance work, which are core to this position
